arguments.description: Parser-owned DSL expression; omit when resuming with continuation.
arguments.name: expression

arguments.description: Requested page size, subject to server and transport bounds.
arguments.name: limit

arguments.description: Declared result projection such as session-summary or cost-rollup.
arguments.name: projection

arguments.description: Opaque token from the preceding response; send alone.
arguments.name: continuation

arguments.expression: actions where action:file_edit AND path:polylogue/archive/query | sort by time desc | limit 20

examples.result_note: An exhaustive page of action rows with object/evidence refs, one result_ref, and a continuation when more rows exist.
examples.title: Find recent edits under the query subsystem

examples.result_note: A session result set suitable for a declared cost-rollup projection; coverage still governs completeness.
examples.title: Select a recent provider cohort for a cost audit

transactions.name: query
transactions.purpose: Execute the real expression DSL or a declared typed plan and return a bounded, semantics-labelled result set.

arguments.description: Stable object, evidence, result-set, or URI reference.
arguments.name: ref

arguments.description: Declared projection/view for the referenced object.
arguments.name: view

arguments.description: Page size for collection-like or recursive reads.
arguments.name: limit

arguments.description: Opaque token from the preceding read response; send alone.
arguments.name: continuation

examples.result_note: A bounded chronicle page retaining message/block evidence refs and the same result_ref across continuation pages.
examples.title: Read a session chronicle

transactions.name: read
transactions.purpose: Read a stable URI/object/evidence ref through a declared view, including topology that the in-flight declarations still call graph.

arguments.description: One exact object/evidence/URI reference.
arguments.name: ref

arguments.description: Optional declared projection of that exact object.
arguments.name: projection

examples.result_note: One object with its canonical ref and provenance; absence is explicit rather than an empty ranked result.
examples.title: Resolve the exact evidence block behind a claim

transactions.name: get
transactions.purpose: Resolve one exact stable identity without search or ranking ambiguity.

arguments.description: Declared explanation subject: query, field, value, ref, capability, result, or recovery.
arguments.name: subject

arguments.description: Query expression to parse and lower when subject=query.
arguments.name: expression

arguments.description: Object/ref whose authority or addressing needs explanation.
arguments.name: ref

arguments.expression: observed-events where kind:tool_finished AND handler:shell | group by status | count
arguments.subject: query

examples.result_note: Parser-owned AST/lowering metadata, selected unit, result semantics, and correction guidance without executing the query.
examples.title: Inspect parser and lowering behavior

transactions.name: explain
transactions.purpose: Explain query grammar, fields, values, lowering, result semantics, refs, capabilities, or recovery before guessing.

arguments.description: Context intent such as resume, postmortem, prior-art, or coordination.
arguments.name: intent

arguments.description: Parser-owned cohort expression that constrains source material.
arguments.name: query

arguments.description: Upper bound for compiled context, not a claim-completeness limit.
arguments.name: budget_tokens

arguments.description: Existing result set to compile without rerunning discovery.
arguments.name: result_ref

arguments.query: sessions where repo:polylogue AND NOT tag:complete

examples.result_note: A bounded context snapshot plus receipt describing selected refs, omissions, policy, and budget use.
examples.title: Compile a resume packet

transactions.name: context
transactions.purpose: Compile a bounded, policy-gated context image with receipts and evidence refs for resumption or investigation.

arguments.description: Status domain such as archive, sources, embeddings, coordination, or operation.
arguments.name: scope

arguments.description: Named status facets requested from that scope.
arguments.name: include

arguments.description: Specific operation/receipt/object ref for status lookup.
arguments.name: ref

examples.result_note: Archive identity, selected source coverage, freshness/readiness state, and explicit degraded reasons.
examples.title: Establish archive authority before making a broad claim

transactions.name: status
transactions.purpose: Report archive identity, readiness, freshness, coverage, coordination, embeddings, and governed operation state.

arguments.description: Declared reversible write operation.
arguments.name: operation

arguments.description: Exact mutation target.
arguments.name: target_ref

arguments.description: Typed operation payload.
arguments.name: value

arguments.description: Optimistic conflict guard where the operation declares one.
arguments.name: expected_generation

examples.result_note: A mutation receipt with actor, target, effect identity, and resulting generation; no destructive confirmation is invented.
examples.title: Add a review tag through the governed write chokepoint

transactions.name: write
transactions.purpose: Apply one declaration-owned reversible mutation with actor, target, conflict policy, and receipt.

arguments.description: Exact assertion-candidate identity.
arguments.name: candidate_ref

arguments.description: Declared judgment decision.
arguments.name: decision

arguments.description: Evidence-grounded judgment rationale.
arguments.name: reason

arguments.description: Conflict guard for concurrent review.
arguments.name: expected_generation

arguments.reason: The cited result set does not cover the claimed source cohort.

examples.result_note: A judgment receipt that leaves candidate history intact and reports conflicts explicitly.
examples.title: Defer a candidate pending stronger evidence

transactions.name: judge
transactions.purpose: Accept, reject, defer, or supersede an assertion candidate while preserving candidate and judgment provenance.

arguments.description: Saved-query or recipe ref.
arguments.name: ref

arguments.description: Typed recipe parameters.
arguments.name: arguments

arguments.description: Opaque token from the preceding run response; send alone.
arguments.name: continuation

examples.result_note: A result_ref and receipt for the declared recipe; mutation authority is never gained from the recipe wrapper.
examples.title: Run a saved read-only cost audit

transactions.name: run
transactions.purpose: Execute a saved query or governed recipe ref; any nested mutation inherits its own role and confirmation policy.

arguments.description: Declared maintenance operation.
arguments.name: operation

arguments.description: preview, execute, status, or reconcile.
arguments.name: phase

arguments.description: Bound target selector used during preview.
arguments.name: target

arguments.description: Preview receipt supplied unchanged for execution.
arguments.name: preview_ref

arguments.description: Short-lived token bound to actor, archive, operation version, expiry, target set, and preview digest.
arguments.name: confirmation_token

examples.result_note: A non-mutating preview receipt with target digest and the data required to obtain a bound confirmation token.
examples.title: Preview an index rebuild before authorization

transactions.name: operate
transactions.purpose: Preview, authorize, execute, inspect, and reconcile administrative maintenance through preview-bound confirmation.
